name: Ao Run
description: Ao Run, also known as the Dragon King of the Eastern Sea, is a prominent figure in Chinese mythology, holding dominion over the vast Eastern Sea and its marine inhabitants. As one of the Dragon Kings, he is responsible for controlling the weather patterns, particularly rainfall, which is vital for agriculture and the prosperity of the land. Ao Run embodies both the benevolent and potentially destructive forces of nature, reflecting the dualistic nature often associated with dragons in Chinese culture. He is often depicted as a regal figure, possessing immense power and authority, and is frequently sought after for blessings and assistance in matters related to the sea and weather. His influence extends beyond the physical realm, as he is also believed to hold sway over the spiritual well-being of those who live near the coastlines. Offerings and prayers are commonly made to Ao Run to ensure favorable conditions and protection from maritime disasters. He is a significant figure in Daoist and Buddhist influenced folk religion.
subtitle: Dragon King of the Eastern Sea

keyMyths.title: The Stolen Ruyi Jingu Bang
keyMyths.description: One of the most famous stories involving Ao Run concerns Sun Wukong, the Monkey King. Sun Wukong, seeking a weapon of immense power, visited Ao Run's underwater palace. Ao Run, attempting to rid himself of the troublesome guest, offered Sun Wukong various weapons, all of which the Monkey King deemed too light. Finally, Sun Wukong discovered the Ruyi Jingu Bang, a magical staff that could change size at will, which originally belonged to Yu the Great. Ao Run, realizing the power of the staff and the audacity of Sun Wukong, was angered by the theft, leading to a conflict between the two. This myth highlights Ao Run's role as a guardian of powerful artifacts and the consequences of disrespecting the Dragon Kings.
keyMyths.source: Wu Cheng'en, Journey to the West

keyMyths.title: Ao Run's control over the tides
keyMyths.description: Ao Run's influence extends to controlling the tides of the Eastern Sea. It is believed that he commands the ebb and flow of the waters, dictating the rhythm of the ocean. Fishermen and sailors would often pray to Ao Run for favorable tides, ensuring safe passage and bountiful catches. The understanding of his power over the tides was crucial for coastal communities, as it directly impacted their livelihoods and their ability to navigate the seas. Disrespecting Ao Run could result in turbulent tides and dangerous conditions, emphasizing the importance of maintaining a respectful relationship with the Dragon King.
keyMyths.source: Werner, E. T. C. Myths and Legends of China.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Rivers
keyMyths.description: In some accounts, Ao Run and the other Dragon Kings are credited with the creation of rivers. They used their powers to carve pathways through the land, guiding water from the mountains to the sea. This act of creation is seen as essential for sustaining life and agriculture. By establishing these waterways, the Dragon Kings ensured a steady supply of water for irrigation and transportation, contributing to the prosperity of the land. This myth emphasizes the Dragon Kings' role as benefactors of humanity and the importance of water as a life-giving resource.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
